memo.execSummary: Lunar Investments' Faheem valuation model rates Jahez International Company (Tadawul: 6017) {calc.rating} with a DCF-anchored target price of {calc.targetPrice} per share against a {calc.currentPrice} close, {calc.upside} implied upside, for a base-case 4-year IRR of {calc.irrBase}, comfortably inside the scenario-weighted expected return of {calc.weightedReturn} against Lunar's {calc.hurdle} mandate hurdle. The thesis rests on Jahez's transition from a single-platform KSA delivery app into a diversified quick-commerce and logistics group: FY2025 GMV of {fy25.gmv} (+{calc.gmvGrowthFy25} YoY) and net revenue of {fy25.net_revenue} (+{calc.netRevGrowthFy25} YoY) were delivered against a headline profit contraction, net income compressed {calc.netIncomeDeclineFy25Magnitude} YoY to {fy25.net_income}, driven by {fy25.one_off} of Q4-2025 one-off items (Red Color impairment, Marn goodwill impairment, Others-segment ECL, Snoonu deal costs) layered on take-rate pressure from the Keeta-led price war, and Q1-2026 printed a {calc.q1LossMagnitude} net loss. Adjusted EBITDA margin contracted ~{calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps to {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} (FY2024A: {fy24.adj_ebitda_margin}), and gross margin, the closest disclosed proxy for contribution margin per order, fell to {fy25.gross_margin} from {fy24.gross_margin}, both consistent with a business absorbing competitive intensity rather than one in structural decline. The compliance screen passes ({calc.debtRatio} debt/market-cap, {calc.cashRatio} cash/market-cap, both under the 33% AAOIFI threshold) and the balance sheet carries {calc.netCash} of net cash. This memo is advisory: the Investment Committee decides.

thesisPillars.title: Logistics diversification de-risks the single-platform delivery model
thesisPillars.body: The Snoonu acquisition (Q4-2025 consolidation) is the strategic pivot: FY2025 Snoonu GMV of {fy25.snoonu_gmv} contributed {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} to Group GMV from October, and delivered {fy25.snoonu_adj_ebitda} of profitable adjusted EBITDA in its own right, the Logistics segment reached {fy25.segment_logistics_net_revenue} of FY2025 net revenue. Group orders grew {calc.ordersGrowthFy25} YoY to {fy25.orders}, and GMV {calc.gmvGrowthFy25} to {fy25.gmv}, the platform is still winning volume even as the KSA delivery wars compress unit economics. Diversification beyond single-app KSA food delivery is the structural offset to the price-war risk quantified below.

thesisPillars.title: Margin recovery is underway, not derailed, despite a headline loss-making quarter
thesisPillars.body: FY2025 adjusted EBITDA of {fy25.adj_ebitda} ({fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} margin) stepped down from FY2024A's {fy24.adj_ebitda} ({fy24.adj_ebitda_margin}) almost entirely on the {fy25.one_off} of identified Q4 one-offs, impairments and deal costs, not operating deterioration, and Q1-2026 adjusted EBITDA of {q1_26.adj_ebitda} shows the operating engine still generating positive cash profit through the net-loss quarter. The scenario-weighted return of {calc.weightedReturn} already prices in the risk that margin recovery slips, and it still clears the {calc.hurdle} hurdle.

thesisPillars.title: A Compliance-compliant, asset-light, net-cash balance sheet fits the mandate cleanly
thesisPillars.body: Jahez screens {calc.complianceStatus} on both AAOIFI-style ratios, interest-bearing debt at {calc.debtRatio} of market cap and cash at {calc.cashRatio}, both inside the 33% threshold, while carrying {calc.netCash} of net cash against {q1_26.islamic_facilities_loans} of Islamic facilities and loans (Q1 2026 Financial Statements). The business model requires minimal fixed capital, and the Tadawul-listed public equity satisfies Lunar's liquidity requirement for the position (Lunar IC Charter, p.4).

memo.companyIndustry: Jahez operates in Saudi Arabia's quick-commerce and food-delivery market against HungerStation, the well-funded new entrant Keeta, and a wave of further well-capitalised players, Rabbit, the Ninja unicorn, and Dingdong, competing primarily on delivery-fee discounting rather than product differentiation (Industry & News Pack · Saudi Quick-Commerce, p.2). The competitive response has been consolidation and diversification: Jahez's Q4-2025 acquisition of logistics platform Snoonu extends the group beyond single-vertical food delivery into broader last-mile logistics, a segment that posted {fy25.segment_logistics_net_revenue} of FY2025 net revenue. Group take rate rose to {fy25.take_rate} in FY2025 from {fy24.take_rate} in FY2024 even as GOV grew to {fy25.gov}, commission monetisation is holding up under pressure, though the price war (Industry & News Pack, p.4) remains the dominant near-term swing factor for sector margins. This memo does not assert a specific market-share position for any player absent a disclosed source.

financialAnalysis.unitEconomics: Orders grew from {fy23.orders} (FY2023A) to {fy24.orders} (FY2024A) to {fy25.orders} (FY2025A, +{calc.ordersGrowthFy25} YoY), against average order value of {fy23.aov}, {fy24.aov} and {fy25.aov} respectively, GMV compounded from {fy23.gmv} to {fy24.gmv} to {fy25.gmv}. Take rate (commission revenue / GMV) rose from {fy23.take_rate} to {fy24.take_rate} to {fy25.take_rate}, lifting commission revenue to {fy25.commission_revenue} even as net revenue growth decelerated to +{calc.netRevGrowthFy25}, the gap between a rising take rate and slowing net-revenue growth is the price-war signature: Jahez is monetising a larger share of a GMV base that is itself growing more slowly.
financialAnalysis.operatingLeverage: Operating leverage cuts both ways in this print. FY2024A's adjusted EBITDA margin of {fy24.adj_ebitda_margin} on {fy24.net_revenue} of net revenue gave way to {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} on {fy25.net_revenue} in FY2025A, a ~{calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps contraction, as depreciation & amortisation grew faster than revenue ({fy24.dna} to {fy25.dna}, reflecting the expanding IFRS-16 right-of-use base) and the {fy25.one_off} of one-offs hit the P&L below the EBITDA line. Segment detail shows the KSA delivery platform still carrying the group: KSA adjusted EBITDA of {fy25.segment_ksa_adj_ebitda} on {fy25.segment_ksa_net_revenue} of net revenue, while Non-KSA, Logistics and Others remain sub-scale or loss-making. The operating-leverage thesis is that fixed platform and logistics costs, once absorbed, let incremental GMV drop through at a materially higher margin than the FY2025A print, which is why the forecast rebuilds margin toward the FY2024A level by the terminal year rather than assuming a permanent step-down.
financialAnalysis.fcfProfile: Free cash flow to the firm is forecast-only in this model, FY2023A-FY2025A capex is not separately disclosed, so no actual-year FCFF feeds the DCF (capex and ΔNWC are documented analyst assumptions; see workbook Assumptions tab). On the explicit forecast, FCFF builds from a depressed FY2026E base as EBITDA margin recovers on the path described above, discounted at a WACC of {calc.wacc} with a {calc.terminalGrowth} terminal growth rate. The DCF sums the explicit-period present value plus a Gordon terminal value into an enterprise value that, net of {calc.netCash} of net cash, implies {calc.targetPrice} per share; full year-by-year FCFF detail is in the workbook's DCF tab.

memo.valuation: The DCF (FCFF, WACC built bottom-up from a {calc.riskFreeRate} Saudi risk-free rate plus a {calc.beta} comp-set beta at a {calc.equityRiskPremium} equity risk premium, giving a {calc.costOfEquity} cost of equity and a {calc.wacc} WACC) anchors the base case at {calc.targetPrice} per share against Gordon terminal growth of {calc.terminalGrowth}, {calc.upside} above the {calc.currentPrice} close. Trading-comps triangulation (EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA and P/E across Talabat, DoorDash and Delivery Hero, Market Data & Comparables Snapshot, p.4) spans a wide {calc.compsMin} to {calc.compsMax} implied-value field (median {calc.compsMedian}), reflecting the gap between Jahez's KSA-scale multiples and the richly-priced US growth comps; the DCF sits toward the lower half of that range, anchored to Jahez's own projected cash flows rather than borrowed US multiples, and is the primary valuation methodology this memo relies on. Scenario-weighted value per share of {calc.weightedPerShare} (25% bull at {calc.perShareBull}, 50% base, 25% bear at {calc.perShareBear}) confirms the base case is not a knife-edge result, the {calc.weightedReturn} probability-weighted expected return clears Lunar's {calc.hurdle} hurdle with room.
memo.riskIntro: The quantified risk register below scores each identified risk on a 1-5 probability x 1-5 impact basis, rolling up to a composite, peak-weighted score of {calc.riskScore} out of 10. The dominant risk is price-war margin compression from Keeta's well-funded discounting, the same dynamic already visible in the {calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps FY2025A EBITDA-margin contraction and the {calc.q1LossMagnitude} Q1-2026A net loss, followed by the arrival of further well-capitalised entrants. Both are structural, sector-wide pressures rather than Jahez-specific execution failures, and both are explicitly reflected in the bear-case scenario ({calc.irrBear} IRR, {calc.perShareBear} per share) rather than assumed away.
memo.mandateFit: Against the Lunar Investment Mandate: the scenario-weighted expected return of {calc.weightedReturn} clears the {calc.hurdle} IRR hurdle (Lunar IC Charter, p.3) with the base case alone at {calc.irrBase}; a new Jahez position would be sized within the mandate's 10% single-name concentration cap (Lunar IC Charter, p.4), subject to final ticket sizing at the Investment Committee's discretion; and the position is publicly listed on Tadawul (6017) with active daily trading, satisfying the liquidity requirement for public-equity allocations (Lunar IC Charter, p.4). The bear case ({calc.irrBear} IRR) would breach the {calc.hurdle} hurdle in isolation, the mandate-fit case rests on the {calc.weightedReturn} probability-weighted return, not the base case alone, and the Investment Committee should weigh that sensitivity explicitly.
memo.compliance: Jahez passes Lunar's AAOIFI/Tadawul-methodology compliance screen on both financial-ratio tests: interest-bearing debt, {q1_26.islamic_facilities_loans} of Islamic facilities and loans (Q1 2026 Financial Statements), sits at {calc.debtRatio} of market capitalisation, and cash & interest-bearing securities, {q1_26.cash} of cash (Q1 2026 Financial Statements; interest-bearing securities are not separately disclosed, so the screen is run on cash alone), sits at {calc.cashRatio}, both comfortably inside the 33% AAOIFI leverage/liquidity threshold. Including IFRS-16 lease liabilities ({q1_26.lease_liabilities}) in the stricter memo view still passes at {calc.leaseInclRatio}. Non-eligible income is not separately disclosed in the filings; per AAOIFI practice the position is screened via the business-activity test instead, food delivery and logistics is an eligible business activity, financed via Islamic facilities, rather than a fabricated income percentage. Overall Compliance screen: {calc.complianceStatus}.
memo.catalysts: Near-term catalysts: Snoonu's first full financial year of consolidation (the FY2025 contribution was {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of Group GMV from an October start; a full FY2026 should show materially more), the path back to positive net income after the {calc.q1LossMagnitude} Q1-2026A loss, and the trajectory of take rate ({fy25.take_rate} in FY2025A) as a read on how much of the Keeta-led price war Jahez can pass through versus absorb. The Investment Committee should treat any further one-off impairment (Q4-2025A already carried {fy25.one_off} across Red Color, Marn goodwill, Others-segment ECL and Snoonu deal costs) as a trigger to re-run the scenario weights, not as one-time noise to dismiss.
monitoringKpis: GMV growth, {fy25.gmv} FY2025A (+{calc.gmvGrowthFy25} YoY); watch for deceleration below the FY2026E-FY2030E forecast path
Take rate, {fy25.take_rate} FY2025A vs {fy24.take_rate} FY2024A; the primary lever on net revenue given a competitive GMV environment
Adjusted EBITDA margin, {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} FY2025A vs {fy24.adj_ebitda_margin} FY2024A; the recovery path is the single biggest swing factor in the valuation
Net income / net loss, {fy25.net_income} FY2025A, {q1_26.net_loss} Q1-2026A; watch for a return to consistent profitability
Cash & net cash position, {q1_26.cash} cash, {calc.netCash} net cash as of Q1-2026; supports the compliance screen and funds continued logistics build-out
Snoonu contribution, {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of FY2025A Group GMV from an October consolidation start; the full-year FY2026 read-through is the key diversification proof point
